title: Houses for Rent in Richmond Heights, OH
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Richmond Heights, Ohio
metaDescription: Looking for houses for rent in Richmond Heights, Ohio? See its location on the Cuyahoga/Lake county line, rent benchmarks, and how to reach our team.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent Richmond Heights ohio
answerBox: Richmond Heights is a Cuyahoga County suburb in the northeast part of the region, near the Lake County line and the city of Willoughby. Rent Finder Cleveland's rental homes are currently concentrated in Cleveland proper rather than Richmond Heights, but tell us what you're looking for and we'll show you comparable voucher-friendly homes we work with in the area.

sections.h2: Renting a house in Richmond Heights, Ohio
body: Richmond Heights sits in the northeast part of Cuyahoga County, near where the county line meets Lake County close to Willoughby. It's one of several northeast Cuyahoga County suburbs, alongside places like Euclid and the Mayfield/Highland Heights area, that developed as residential communities outside Cleveland's city limits.

For renters weighing Richmond Heights against a city-of-Cleveland address, the jurisdictions differ in practical ways: Richmond Heights is its own suburb with its own municipal government, while neighborhoods like Collinwood and Glenville a few miles southwest are part of the City of Cleveland, subject to Cleveland's own inspection rules and, for older housing stock built before 1978, the city's Lead-Safe Certification requirement.

**Rent Finder Cleveland works with 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods, and Richmond Heights isn't currently part of our available homes.** If a Richmond Heights address specifically is what you need, a suburb-focused property manager will have more direct listings there. If your search is flexible on the exact suburb, [tell us what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) — bedrooms, budget, and voucher status — and we'll point you to comparable homes we work with in nearby Cleveland neighborhoods such as Collinwood and Glenville.

sections.h2: What does it cost to rent a house near Richmond Heights?
body: Richmond Heights-specific rents vary by house and condition, but as a Greater Cleveland-area benchmark, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report shows a metro median of about **$1,100** for a 2-bedroom and **$1,350** for a 3-bedroom home. Within our own nearby Cleveland northeast-side selection (Collinwood/44110 area), 2-bedroom rental homes typically run **$750–$1,100** a month and 3-bedroom homes typically run **$1,100–$1,500**, based on our real current listings in that part of the city.

sections.h2: Getting around Richmond Heights and northeast Cuyahoga County
body: Richmond Heights sits near the Cuyahoga/Lake county line, close to Willoughby, and in the same general northeast corridor as Cleveland's Collinwood neighborhoods. The area is also near Mayfield, home to Progressive Corporation's headquarters — one of the region's larger employers — which makes the northeast Cuyahoga County suburbs a common commute destination for that workforce. Like much of eastern Cuyahoga County, Richmond Heights is within Northeast Ohio's lake-effect snow belt, which generally sees more seasonal snowfall than west-side, lakeshore-adjacent parts of the county.

items: **Location:** Northeast Cuyahoga County, near the Lake County line and Willoughby
**Nearby employer:** Progressive Corporation headquarters in Mayfield
**Climate note:** Part of Northeast Ohio's east-side snow belt

body: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept Housing Choice Vouchers; acceptance depends on the individual property owner unless a specific municipality has adopted its own protection ordinance. Richmond Heights is not among the Cuyahoga County suburbs reported to have a local source-of-income protection ordinance, so voucher holders should confirm directly with any specific landlord there.

**Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready**, including our nearby homes in Cleveland's Collinwood and Glenville neighborhoods. The [Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) administers the Housing Choice Voucher program countywide, covering both Cleveland and its suburbs including Richmond Heights, and accepts preliminary applications year-round. Our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) covers the local process in more depth.

If your search includes Cleveland proper, our [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page covers the northeast-side neighborhoods where we work with inventory nearby. You can also compare notes on nearby [Euclid](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-euclid-ohio/) and [Willoughby](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-willoughby-ohio/), or browse our full [suburb-by-suburb guide](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/) to see everywhere we cover.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with houses in Richmond Heights, Ohio?
faq.a: Not currently. Our 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods. Richmond Heights isn't part of our current selection, but tell us what you're looking for and we'll point you to comparable homes we work with nearby in Cleveland.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house near Richmond Heights, Ohio?
faq.a: In our nearby Cleveland Collinwood-area selection, 2-bedroom rental homes typically run $750–$1,100 a month and 3-bedroom homes run $1,100–$1,500. As a broader metro benchmark, Zumper's July 2026 report shows a median of about $1,100 for 2-bedroom and $1,350 for 3-bedroom units.

faq.q: Where is Richmond Heights, Ohio located?
faq.a: Richmond Heights is a Cuyahoga County suburb in the northeast part of the region, near the Lake County line and the city of Willoughby, in the same general corridor as Cleveland's Collinwood neighborhoods.

faq.q: Do landlords in Richmond Heights accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: It depends on the individual landlord — Ohio has no statewide law requiring voucher acceptance, and Richmond Heights isn't reported to have a local source-of-income protection ordinance. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready; CMHA administers the voucher program countywide, including Richmond Heights.
